rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LAP CHAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 24, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff's 1st and 2nd causes of action for discrimination fail because plaintiff has not presented evidence of a prima facie case. Further, defendant has presented evidence of an appropriate reason for plaintiff's termination. Plaintiff has presented no evidence that the reason for the termination was a pretext and that discriminatory animus motivated defendant. The 3rd and 4th causes of action fail because the claims are duplicative of the 1st and 2nd causes of action and are barred by the failure to file a government tort claim and are subject to the workers compensation exclusive remedy. Defendant's proposed order is consistent with the Court's findings. =(302/PJM/ER)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. DANIEL NG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 24, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT DANIEL NG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days to answer. The complaint presents facts distinct from the writ proceeding. The fact that defendant moved out is not a basis for a demurrer because that fact is not on the face of the complaint. The fraud and remaining claims have plead sufficient facts. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. DANIEL NG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 24, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT DANIEL NG'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Strike the request for attorneys fees because the plaintiff is suing in pro per. =(302/PJM/JW)
